original_tool_input.base_case: Over the next 30-60 days the most likely path is a continued corrective phase off the 6/2 swing high (7620.90) that has already broken the 50% fib and re-anchored to the nervous-zone liquidation level near 7240. Price remains above all key SMAs (5.7% over 200dma, 50>200) so the primary uptrend is structurally intact, but momentum, breadth, credit, and sentiment have all rolled over together. Base case: choppy consolidation / further mean reversion toward 7240, with the 50-day SMA (~7213) and ERP-confirmed floor (~7021) as the deeper supports, before the trend resumes only if breadth and credit stabilize.
original_tool_input.trend_regime: Bullish but maturing: 50-day SMA above 200-day SMA with price +0.74% over the 50dma and +5.74% over the 200dma, but the daily/short-term structure has turned lower with a series of lower highs and lower lows since the 6/2 top.
original_tool_input.valuation_bucket: ERP 0.33% (0.0-0.5% valuation ceiling band) - trim bias; Forward PE 20.5x - cautious buying only at strong confluence

signals.bollinger_position: Below middle band, riding toward lower band on the 1-month chart
signals.middle_band_regime: Closed below 20-day SMA after an extended advance - short-term regime shift
signals.intraday_close_position: Bottom third of daily range (close 7266.99 vs range 7265.93-7396.56)

what_changed_today: SPX fell -119.66 (-1.62%) to close 7266.99 in the bottom third of its daily range - a weak, distribution-style close
Active leg fully retraced the prior downside fib target; engine re-anchored the downside Monte Carlo straddle to the nervous-zone liquidation level (7239.85)
Close confirmed below the 20-day SMA, signaling a short-term regime shift after the extended advance
VIX pushed into the elevated >20 regime (22.22), reducing reliability of moving-average support
Fear & Greed dropped sharply into Fear (~27) with multiple sub-indicators at Extreme Fear (breadth/McClellan, safe-haven demand, junk-bond demand)
original_tool_input.narrative_summary: SPX sold off 1.62% to 7266.99, closing weakly in the bottom third of its range and below the 20-day SMA, while VIX climbed into the elevated >20 regime. The longer-term trend is still bullish (50>200, +5.7% over the 200dma), but momentum, breadth, credit, and sentiment have all deteriorated together, with several Fear & Greed components at Extreme Fear. Monte Carlo strongly favors hitting the re-anchored downside (7240) first (~82%) and is not actionable for new longs at any threshold. Posture: defensive patience - hold, protect capital, and wait for re-entry confirmation rather than buying this shallow-to-moderate pullback.
open_questions: Will the 50-day SMA (~7213) and re-anchored 7240 nervous zone hold, or does price extend toward the ERP floor (~7021)?
Does credit keep widening (junk spread spiked to ~1.46% intraday) and confirm escalating risk, or was the spike a transient ex-dividend artifact?
Will VIX spike and reverse to produce a high-quality capitulative re-entry, or does the correction stay orderly without a volatility climax?
original_tool_input.primary_tension: An intact longer-term uptrend (50>200, price still 5.7% above the 200dma) versus a synchronized short-term breakdown in momentum, breadth, credit, and sentiment - structure says 'still a bull,' but the tape, internals, and probabilities say 'defend, don't buy.'
confirming_evidence: 50-day SMA remains above 200-day SMA across the 3M/6M/1Y charts; price still 5.74% above the 200dma - primary uptrend intact
Pullback so far is shallow-to-moderate (-4.6% off the 7620.90 high), not yet a margin-call (10%) or cascade (15%) event
ERP still marginally positive (0.33%) and ERP-confirmed re-entry floor sits well below at ~7021, leaving room before valuation forces trim-only
Put/call rose off complacent ~0.60 lows toward ~0.78, consistent with rising fear from low levels - an early bottoming-watch ingredient (not yet confirmation)

conflicting_evidence.bullish_read: Price is still well above its 200dma and the 50>200 regime is intact, so the trend hasn't structurally broken.
conflicting_evidence.bearish_read: McClellan Summation has rolled over to ~1000 and net new highs/lows are falling toward zero while price sits near highs - narrow, deteriorating participation classic of a topping phase.
conflicting_evidence.framework_rule: When breadth and credit both diverge from price, elevate caution even if price has not yet broken down.

conflicting_evidence.bullish_read: Junk spread absolute level (~1.36%) is still mid-range historically and spikes often reflect ex-dividend artifacts.
conflicting_evidence.bearish_read: Junk spread spiked sharply higher to ~1.46% and the Fear & Greed junk-bond component reads Extreme Fear - credit is diverging from equity strength.
conflicting_evidence.framework_rule: Widening spreads = credit warning; sustained widening well above recent average is escalating caution.

conflicting_evidence.bullish_read: RSI ~42 and MFI ~32 are mid-range/neutral, not yet oversold, so there is no momentum-exhaustion buy signal but also no overbought trim trigger.
conflicting_evidence.bearish_read: On the 1-month chart RSI rolled from ~70 down to low-40s and MFI fell below 50 toward ~32, confirming institutional selling pressure during the decline.
conflicting_evidence.framework_rule: MFI falling below 50 during a decline confirms institutional selling pressure.

conflicting_evidence.bullish_read: VIX at 22 is only modestly elevated and could mean-revert lower, supporting a bounce.
conflicting_evidence.bearish_read: VIX in the >20 regime reduces reliability of moving-average support and widens probability tails; no spike-and-reverse capitulation has occurred yet.
conflicting_evidence.framework_rule: When VIX is above 20, reduce confidence in moving-average support.
